description: Self-eval fixture: scripted multi-session conversations for two users. Exercises personal facts, preferences, pets, diet, employment + location supersession, an opinion arc, cross-session sharing, and cross-user isolation. Designed so the deterministic rule extractor can recover the key facts (so the harness runs with no API key); the LLM extractor additionally captures implicit facts and the opinion arc.

messages.content: Hi! I just moved to Berlin from New York for a new job.
Welcome to Berlin! What's the new job?
I work at Stripe as a backend engineer. Mostly Go and a bit of Ruby.
Nice, payments infra is fun. How's the team?
My dog Biscuit is settling in well too — he loves the big park nearby.
Aww, Biscuit sounds happy!
Planning dinner. I'm vegetarian and allergic to shellfish, so I keep it simple.
Got it — veggie and no shellfish. Any cuisine you prefer?
I really love TypeScript for side projects these days.
TypeScript is great for that.
Big news — I just started at Notion as a product manager! Left the engineering role behind.
Congrats on the move into PM!
By the way, I prefer concise, direct answers — no long preambles.
Understood, I'll keep it brief.
Honestly TypeScript's generics are getting annoying in the big codebase at work.
They can get hairy at scale.
I live in Boston and I work at HubSpot.
Nice, Boston is great. How's HubSpot?
Correction: I live in Seattle now, not Boston — relocated for work.
Thanks for the update — Seattle it is.
I live in Tokyo and I work at Sony on camera firmware.
Tokyo is wonderful. Firmware, nice.
I have a cat named Mochi and I love photography.
Mochi is a great name!
